guidance.operatingMarginGuide: 13.5% non-GAAP for full year; 12.5% for Q2

keyQuotes: AI has created a 'healthy secular tailwind' for the core data platform, while products such as Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code are creating second-order effects by making it easier for customers to complete projects, create pipelines, develop agents, and accelerate migrations. — Sridhar Ramaswamy

Product revenue reached $1.334 billion, up 34% year over year, marking an acceleration from 30% growth in the prior quarter and 26% a year earlier, as well as the company's strongest sequential dollar growth in its history. — Sridhar Ramaswamy
aiCommentary: Management positioned Snowflake as the 'agentic control plane for the enterprise,' with AI products including Snowflake Intelligence and Cortex Code scaling rapidly. CEO Ramaswamy emphasized that AI is driving both direct consumption on the platform and second-order effects — making migrations faster, pipelines easier to build, and agents simpler to deploy.
demandCommentary: Enterprise demand showed clear acceleration, with product revenue growth re-accelerating from 30% in Q4 FY2026 to 34% in Q1 FY2027 — the highest sequential dollar growth in company history. Customer count grew to 13,912 with 616 net new customers added, up 38% YoY, and NRR expanded to 126%.
analystTake: Analyst Q&A centered not on demand concerns but on durability of AI-driven revenue contributions and how Snowflake plans to sustain re-acceleration. Analysts also probed competitive positioning against Databricks and emerging AI-native data startups. Stock dipped slightly after hours despite the beat, suggesting some investors focused on pace of margin expansion.
